title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse — FDIC seizure and contagion
description: Silicon Valley Bank ($209B assets, deposits concentrated in VC-backed startups) collapses on March 10, 2023 after a bank run triggered by a $1.8B bond portfolio loss announcement. FDIC seizes SVB on March 10 and Signature Bank on March 12. Treasury/Fed/FDIC invoke systemic risk exception, guaranteeing all deposits. First Republic Bank ($229B) fails under pressure and is acquired by JPMorgan on May 1.
